county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1887; post-Civil War formation; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Pasco County Clerk (Dade City). Formed 1887 from Hernando. Pre-1887 records in Hernando (and earlier in Alachua). Dade City and New Port Richey area. Pasco County Historical Society and Dade Battlefield Historic State Park. Second Seminole War Battle of Dade (1835) — Dade City named for Major Dade. Greek sponge fishing community at Tarpon Springs area (shared with Pinellas). Pioneer Florida Museum has local materials.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books for Pasco County from 1887 forward; microfilm copies at Florida State Archives

source_profiles.survival_notes: County marriage records from formation; statewide recording began 1927

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1912; 20th-century formation; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Pinellas County Clerk (Clearwater). Formed 1912 from Hillsborough. Pre-1912 records in Hillsborough. St. Petersburg and Clearwater area. Heritage Village (Largo) has local genealogical resources. St. Petersburg Museum of History. Tampa Bay area fishing and tourism industry records. Pinellas County Historical Society. Safety Harbor area has archaeological significance (Tocobaga people). Railroad and tourism development records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1861; antebellum county; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Polk County Clerk (Bartow). Formed 1861 from Brevard and Hillsborough. Central Florida's phosphate mining center. Polk County Historical Association has local collections (Bartow). Lakeland area — Florida Southern College (Frank Lloyd Wright campus). Bok Tower Gardens area. Pre-1861 records in parent counties. Cattle ranching and citrus industry heritage. Winter Haven area pioneer records. Bartow was a key railroad junction.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1849; antebellum county; North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Putnam County Clerk (Palatka). Formed 1849 from Alachua, Marion, and St. Johns. Palatka was a major 19th-century steamboat landing on the St. Johns River. Putnam County Archives and History has local genealogical resources. Ravine Gardens State Park. Pre-1849 records in parent counties. Significant African American community records. Timber and citrus industry. St. Johns River area settlement records. Cross Creek (Marjorie Kinnan Rawlings) area historical records.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1869 courthouse fire destroyed many early records
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1842; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); Panhandle region
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Santa Rosa County Clerk (Milton). Formed 1842 from Escambia. BURNED COUNTY — 1869 courthouse fire destroyed many early records. Pre-1842 records in Escambia. Panhandle county with strong ties to Pensacola area. Blackwater River area. Santa Rosa Historical Society has local materials. Bagdad Village area — historic lumber mill town. Whiting Field Naval Air Station records. Check Florida State Archives for surviving state copies. Many families also in Escambia records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1921; 20th-century formation; South Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Sarasota County Clerk (Sarasota). Formed 1921 from Manatee — a 20th-century county. Pre-1921 records in Manatee (and earlier in Hillsborough). Ringling Museum of Art and Circus archives. Sarasota County Historical Resources has extensive genealogical collections. Newtown (African American community) historical records. Bertha Palmer estate records. Venice area archaeological sites (ancient burial grounds). 1920s Florida land boom transformed the area.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1913; 20th-century formation; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Seminole County Clerk (Sanford). Formed 1913 from Orange. Pre-1913 records in Orange (and earlier in St. Johns as Mosquito County). Named for the Seminole people. Sanford was a major steamboat landing on the St. Johns River and Henry Sanford's colony. Seminole County Historical Society and Museum of Seminole County History have local materials. Celery and citrus farming records. Geneva area pioneer families. Goldsboro (African American community) historical records.

county_metadata.parent_county: Original Florida territory county (from Spanish East Florida)
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1821; original territorial county; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and St. Johns County Clerk (St. Augustine). One of Florida's two original territorial counties (1821), created from Spanish East Florida. St. Augustine is the oldest continuously occupied European settlement in the US (1565). St. Augustine Historical Society has exceptional collections spanning the Spanish, British, and American periods. Cathedral Parish records (Catholic, from 1594). P.K. Yonge Library at UF holds transcribed Spanish colonial records. Flagler College (former Hotel Ponce de Leon) area. Fort Matanzas and Castillo de San Marcos records at NPS. Minorcan community records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1905; 20th-century formation; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and St. Lucie County Clerk (Fort Pierce). Current county formed 1905 from Brevard (a different county from the original St. Lucie, which became Brevard). Pre-1905 records in Brevard. Indian River area citrus industry. Fort Pierce was a Seminole Wars fort. St. Lucie County Historical Society has local materials. A.E. Backus Museum area. Navy UDT-SEAL Museum (Fort Pierce was WWII training site). Florida East Coast Railway records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1853; antebellum county; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Sumter County Clerk (Bushnell). Formed 1853 from Marion and Orange. Dade Battlefield Historic State Park (Second Seminole War). Pre-1853 records in Marion and Orange. Sumter County Historical Society has local materials. The Villages retirement community (1980s+). Bushnell area cattle and citrus heritage. Withlacoochee State Forest area. Coleman area records. Check Florida State Archives for Seminole Wars military records.
